claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API implements scoped evidence separation where WebFetch/WebSearch results are marked as external research and cannot satisfy existing-project fact requirements without project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() classifies evidence as project/external/user/unknown based on record.path, record.url, record.source, and record fields
evidence.why: Core evidence scope classification logic that separates external research from project facts

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() generates PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker when evidence is non-project scopes only
evidence.why: Validation that blocks external-only evidence from satisfying project fact requirements

evidence.summary: PRD readiness check iterates over evidence results, calling projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() for each complete result and adding blockers
evidence.why: Integration point where external-only evidence blocks PRD readiness

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt() includes rule: 'If the demand explicitly asks for external research/fetch/search, actually use WebFetch/WebSearch when available and record those records as scope=external'
evidence.why: Agent prompt contract that requires external research to be marked as scope=external

evidence.summary: executionConfig() normalizes Claude agent_tool_profile to 'research' for research/fetch/web profiles, enabling WebFetch/WebSearch
evidence.why: Provider configuration that enables external research tools while maintaining boundary rules

evidence.summary: Documentation defines three evidence agent protocols (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) and states external evidence can only be background research
evidence.why: Product specification that external evidence cannot prove target project implementation facts

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: 'external evidence can come from WebFetch/WebSearch or external documents, but can only be used as background research, and cannot independently prove that the target project already has fields, APIs, state machines, or data flows'
evidence.why: Product requirement that external-only evidence cannot satisfy project fact claims

evidence.summary: Test verifies agent evidence without explicit scope is blocked with EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED error code
evidence.why: Test coverage ensuring scope declaration is enforced

evidence.summary: Test verifies Claude research profile sets agent_tool_profile to 'research' and does not restrict tools
evidence.why: Test coverage for WebFetch/WebSearch capability configuration

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult() checks evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope() and sets status to blocked with EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED when scope is missing
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement that all evidence must declare scope before acceptance

assumptions: WebFetch/WebSearch tools are available in the Claude provider environment when agent_tool_profile is 'research' or 'full'
Agent providers respect the agent prompt contract and mark WebFetch/WebSearch results as scope=external
The boundary snapshot mechanism (buildBoundarySnapshot, diffBoundarySnapshots) correctly detects project mutations outside allowed artifact roots
risks: If agent providers ignore scope declaration rules, external research could be mistaken for project facts
If WebFetch/WebSearch tools are unavailable despite 'research' profile, agents might report missing instead of using external research
If boundary audit has false positives or false negatives, project mutations could go undetected or allowed mutations could be incorrectly blocked

result.notes: The codebase implements a complete evidence scope separation system: scope classification (project/external/user/unknown), validation that blocks external-only evidence from satisfying project facts, agent prompt rules requiring WebFetch/WebSearch results to be marked as external, and test coverage for scope enforcement and research profile configuration. The product doctrine explicitly states external evidence cannot prove project implementation facts.
